WaitDelayerDefinition.description: Configuration object for selecting and parameterizing a `WaitDelayer` from the registry.

interval.description: Base interval in milliseconds passed to the delayer factory.

Schema.description: Parameters for the in-memory FIFO queue, including message expiration and receive timeout

expire.description: Number of seconds before droping message

maxConsumers.description:
Max number of queue consumers
Queue will auto increase to this max number if queue is loaded
and it will decrease to just one consumer if no messages are available

timeout.description: Number of seconds to wait for receiving new message

workerDelayer.description: Delayer between two failed attempts to process messages

workerParallelism.description: Define if worker should process multi message received in //

Schema.title: QueueService

BinaryFileInfo<{}>.description:
One Binary instance — single-cardinality binary attribute on a CoreModel.

Tagged as a Behavior so the compiler picks it up via the same path as every
other `@WebdaBehavior` class. Its `@Action`-decorated methods are exposed
as model-scoped operations (`<Model>.<Attribute>.<Action>`) and routed by
`RESTOperationsTransport.exposeBehaviorRoutes`.

Constructor takes no required arguments because the transformer-emitted
`__hydrateBehaviors` calls `new Binary()` with zero args before stamping
the parent reference into `WEBDA_STORAGE["__parent__"]`. Legacy 2-arg
usage (`new Binary(attribute, model)`) is still supported for direct
callers and tests; when present, the constructor eagerly resolves the
service and copies the existing model attribute value so callers can call
`isEmpty()` and `hash`/`size` immediately.

challenge.description:
Will be computed by the service

hash of the content prefixed by 'WEBDA'

metadata.description: Metadatas stored along with the binary

optional.description:

forcing UI to push the attribute

readonly.description:
To indicate a property that will be set by server,
similar to

Binaries<{ location: { lat: number; lng:.description:
Type-level alias for `BinariesImpl<T>`.

Models declare `photos: Binaries<...>` rather than the more verbose
`photos: BinariesImpl<...>`; the compiler resolves this alias to the
`BinariesImpl` class declaration, picks up the `@WebdaBehavior` JSDoc
tag there, and registers the attribute as a Behavior. The runtime
shape (push/splice/index access plus `attach`, `attachChallenge`, `get`,
`getUrl`, `deleteAt`, `setMetadata`, `upload`) comes from
`BinariesImpl` directly.

length.description: Gets or sets the length of the array. This is a number one higher than the highest index in the array.

BinaryFileInfo<{}>.description:
One Binary instance — single-cardinality binary attribute on a CoreModel.

Tagged as a Behavior so the compiler picks it up via the same path as every
other `@WebdaBehavior` class. Its `@Action`-decorated methods are exposed
as model-scoped operations (`<Model>.<Attribute>.<Action>`) and routed by
`RESTOperationsTransport.exposeBehaviorRoutes`.

Constructor takes no required arguments because the transformer-emitted
`__hydrateBehaviors` calls `new Binary()` with zero args before stamping
the parent reference into `WEBDA_STORAGE["__parent__"]`. Legacy 2-arg
usage (`new Binary(attribute, model)`) is still supported for direct
callers and tests; when present, the constructor eagerly resolves the
service and copies the existing model attribute value so callers can call
`isEmpty()` and `hash`/`size` immediately.

Input.description: Course linking a teacher, students, and a classroom.

students.description:
Link to a collection of objects including some additional data

Uses composition instead of extending Array for better maintainability,
performance, and type safety. Provides array-like interface through
explicit methods and iterators.

It does not require to have a symetric relation on the other side
The array contains uuid of the linked objects and additional properties defined as K

Sample usage:
```typescript
class MyModel extends AbstractCoreModel {
  _links: ModelLinksArray<OtherModel, { custom: string }>;
}
```

items.description: A ModelRef augmented with custom properties K, exposing them both at runtime and in JSON.

Uses composition instead of extending Array for better maintainability,
performance, and type safety.

It does not require to have a symetric relation on the other side
The array contains uuid of the linked objects

Promise<any>.description: Represents the completion of an asynchronous operation

writable.description:
Is `true` if it is safe to call `writable.write()`, which means
the stream has not been destroyed, errored, or ended.

context.description: OperationContext is used when call to an operation
param: T type of input for this context
U type of output for this context
P type of parameters for this context

_promises.description: Contain all registered promises to this context

caches.description: Get the caches linked to the context
